event_text: Henry II becomes king of East Francia and later Holy Roman Emperor.
Conrad II begins the Salian dynasty.
Henry IV performs the Walk to Canossa during conflict with Pope Gregory VII.
The Concordat of Worms helps settle the Investiture Controversy.
Frederick Barbarossa becomes king of Germany.
Henry the Lion is outlawed and his duchies are broken up.
Frederick Barbarossa dies during the Third Crusade.
Frederick II issues the Confoederatio cum principibus ecclesiasticis, strengthening church princes.
The Rhenish League forms to protect trade and peace along the Rhine.
Rudolf I of Habsburg is elected king, ending the Great Interregnum.
The Golden Bull establishes rules for electing the Holy Roman Emperor.
Sigismund becomes king of the Romans.
The Council of Constance opens and later ends the Western Schism.
Johannes Gutenberg’s movable-type printing develops in Mainz.
Martin Luther publishes his Ninety-five Theses at Wittenberg.
Luther appears before the Diet of Worms.
The German Peasants War begins in south-western German-speaking lands.
The Augsburg Confession presents Lutheran beliefs.
The Peace of Augsburg recognises Lutheranism within the empire.
The Defenestration of Prague triggers the crisis that begins the Thirty Years War.
The Peace of Westphalia ends the Thirty Years War.
Frederick I crowns himself King in Prussia.
Frederick the Great becomes King of Prussia.
Frederick II invades Saxony, opening the European phase of the Seven Years War.
The Holy Roman Empire is dissolved after Napoleonic victories.
The Battle of Leipzig defeats Napoleon’s forces in Germany.
The German Confederation is created after the Congress of Vienna.
The Zollverein customs union begins operating.
Revolutionary unrest spreads through the German states, demanding liberal reform and national unity.
Otto von Bismarck becomes minister-president of Prussia.
Prussia defeats Austria in the Austro-Prussian War.
The Franco-Prussian War leads to German unification.
The German Empire is proclaimed at Versailles.
Bismarck introduces Anti-Socialist Laws.
Germany introduces pioneering national health insurance.
The Year of the Three Emperors sees Wilhelm I, Frederick III and Wilhelm II rule in one year.
Bismarck resigns after conflict with Kaiser Wilhelm II.
The Schlieffen Plan shapes German military planning.
Germany fights in the First World War.
The German Revolution ends the monarchy and leads to the Weimar Republic.
The Treaty of Versailles imposes terms on Germany after the First World War.
Hyperinflation and the Ruhr occupation destabilise the Weimar Republic.
The Great Depression severely affects Germany.
Adolf Hitler becomes chancellor and Nazi dictatorship begins.
The Nuremberg Laws strip German Jews of citizenship rights.
Kristallnacht marks a major escalation of anti-Jewish violence.
Germany starts and fights the Second World War in Europe.
Germany invades the Soviet Union in Operation Barbarossa.
The Wannsee Conference coordinates the Holocaust.
Germany surrenders and is divided into occupation zones.
The Berlin Blockade leads to the Berlin Airlift.
West Germany and East Germany are established.
The East German uprising is suppressed by Soviet forces.
West Germany joins NATO.
West Germany becomes a founding member of the European Economic Community.
The Berlin Wall is built.
The Élysée Treaty formalises Franco-German cooperation.
Willy Brandt becomes chancellor and begins Ostpolitik.
Munich hosts the Olympic Games, marked by a terrorist attack on Israeli athletes.
The Berlin Wall falls after peaceful revolution in East Germany.
Germany is reunified.
Berlin is chosen as the seat of government of reunified Germany.
The German government begins moving from Bonn to Berlin.
Germany adopts euro banknotes and coins.
Angela Merkel becomes Germany’s first female chancellor.
Germany receives large numbers of refugees during the European migrant crisis.
The COVID-19 pandemic affects Germany.
Olaf Scholz becomes chancellor after the federal election.
